user_message: Working on Lucerne Publishing's forecast prep. Got the support ticket -- help me assess the risk.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 16:40 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Lucerne Publishing

Our team encountered some challenges with forecast prep. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 12:35 EST
Subject: Re: Re: forecast prep Timeline

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 12:52 EST
Subject: Re: Re: forecast prep Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-M trap (No native 'multi-thread synthesis' primitive); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-M failure mode (No native 'multi-thread synthesis' primitive)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-M -- No native 'multi-thread synthesis' primitive
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
